topic: bangladeshi hajj pilgrims die in saudi arabia
headline_marked: Nearly 50,000 Bangladeshi Hajj pilgrims return home, [[49 pilgrims die]] in Saudi Arabia
headline: Nearly 50,000 Bangladeshi Hajj pilgrims return home, 49 pilgrims die in Saudi Arabia
summary: As the Hajj pilgrimage concludes, thousands of Bangladeshi pilgrims are returning home. Tragically, 49 pilgrims have died in Saudi Arabia during the holy journey.
details: Nearly 50,000 Bangladeshi pilgrims have returned to Bangladesh after completing their Hajj journey in Saudi Arabia. The pilgrims arrived back in the country via various flights.
During their stay in Saudi Arabia for the Hajj, a total of 49 Bangladeshi pilgrims passed away. The causes of death have not been specified.
The Saudi authorities and Bangladeshi officials in Saudi Arabia facilitated the Hajj pilgrimage for a large number of devotees from Bangladesh this year.
caption:
Almost 50,000 Bangladeshi Hajj pilgrims have returned home. Sadly, 49 pilgrims died in Saudi Arabia during the pilgrimage.

Nearly 50,000 Bangladeshi pilgrims have returned to Bangladesh after completing their Hajj journey in Saudi Arabia. The pilgrims arrived back in the country via various flights.

During their stay in Saudi Arabia for the Hajj, a total of 49 Bangladeshi pilgrims passed away. The causes of death have not been specified.

The Saudi authorities and Bangladeshi officials in Saudi Arabia facilitated the Hajj pilgrimage for a large number of devotees from Bangladesh this year.

#Hajj #Bangladesh #SaudiArabia #Pilgrimage #News

Source: Dhaka Tribune
tweet: Nearly 50,000 Bangladeshi Hajj pilgrims have returned home. Tragically, 49 pilgrims passed away in Saudi Arabia during the holy journey. #Hajj #Bangladesh

topic: gas cylinder explosion injures 5 in narayanganj
headline_marked: [[Gas cylinder explosion]] in Narayanganj leaves five, including a family of four, with severe burns
headline: Gas cylinder explosion in Narayanganj leaves five, including a family of four, with severe burns
summary: A gas leak from a cylinder caused a powerful explosion in a Narayanganj home, severely burning five people. The injured have been transferred to the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute for critical care.
details: A gas cylinder leak led to a devastating explosion in Chandpur, Bandar Upazila, Narayanganj, early Thursday morning. The blast injured five individuals, including four members of the same family who resided in the rented apartment.

Investigators suspect that gas had accumulated inside the room due to a leak in the cylinder's connection pipe. The explosion occurred when the family members attempted to ignite a flame, possibly for cooking or lighting, spreading fire rapidly.

Neighbors reported hearing a loud blast followed by the screams of the injured as they emerged from the house. Local residents assisted in transporting the severely burned individuals to the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute in Dhaka via ambulance.

According to Dr. Shawon Bin Rahman, an RMO at the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute, the victims sustained extensive burns. Sultana Begum suffered burns on 90% of her body, Sayem on 77%, Mim on 41%, Abdul Mannan on 30%, and Ali on 8%. Some patients also suffered respiratory tract injuries.

The fire service arrived at the scene and brought the situation under control. They are currently investigating the precise cause of the explosion. The family had been renting a two-room flat in a one-story building, utilizing both piped gas and an LPG cylinder for their needs.
caption:
Five people, including a family of four, were severely burned in a gas cylinder explosion in Narayanganj. The incident occurred early Thursday morning due to a gas leak.

A gas cylinder leak led to a devastating explosion in Chandpur, Bandar Upazila, Narayanganj, early Thursday morning. The blast injured five individuals, including four members of the same family who resided in the rented apartment.

Investigators suspect that gas had accumulated inside the room due to a leak in the cylinder's connection pipe. The explosion occurred when the family members attempted to ignite a flame, possibly for cooking or lighting, spreading fire rapidly.

Neighbors reported hearing a loud blast followed by the screams of the injured as they emerged from the house. Local residents assisted in transporting the severely burned individuals to the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute in Dhaka via ambulance.

According to Dr. Shawon Bin Rahman, an RMO at the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute, the victims sustained extensive burns. Sultana Begum suffered burns on 90% of her body, Sayem on 77%, Mim on 41%, Abdul Mannan on 30%, and Ali on 8%. Some patients also suffered respiratory tract injuries.

The fire service arrived at the scene and brought the situation under control. They are currently investigating the precise cause of the explosion. The family had been renting a two-room flat in a one-story building, utilizing both piped gas and an LPG cylinder for their needs.

#Narayanganj #GasExplosion #BurnInjuries #Bangladesh #Accident

Source: Dhaka Tribune, Jamuna TV, The Daily Star, Somoy News
